name: Suspicious Execution of GRP File Conversion
notes:
This rule detects suspicious execution of .grp file conversion.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
SIGMA Title: Suspicious GrpConv Execution
SIGMA Description: Detects the suspicious execution of a utility to convert Windows 3.x .grp files or for persistence purposes by malicious software or actors


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%grpconv.exe -o%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%grpconv -o%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Execution of GRP File Conversion on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%grpconv.exe -o%' OR LOWER("Command") LIKE '%grpconv -o%' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Execution of GRP File Conversion
Event description: Detects suspicious execution of .grp file conversion.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
